Title: OLASS – Offenders in custody
Description: Use this code only if you are a provider contracted to deliver learning to offenders in custodial establishments through the Offenders’ Learning and Skills Service (OLASS). This will enable the tracking and matching of offender learning and the continuity of learning aims when moving from offender learning in custody into the community.

Title: OLASS – Offenders in the community
Description: Use this code only if you are delivering learning to offenders under supervision, or serving a sentence in the community. In order for providers to utilise this code, a referral to learning provision must have been made by either the National Probation Service or Youth Offending Team.

Title: European Social Fund 2007/13
Description: This code is for 2007/13 ESF Co-financing activity only. It should be used in combination with code 95 in L39 (Destination) to identify where an ESF participant has progressed from ESF Co-financed activity into a mainstream or other learning programme with the same provider. See the ILR Summarisation Rules document which is available via the ESF Intranet for more detail.

Title: Apprenticeship supported or funded by ESF
Description: This code should be used where ESF funding for 2007/13 is being used to wholly fund apprenticeships or where ESF funding is being used to support the delivery of mainstream apprenticeships.

Title: Proxy Learning Aim
Description: This code should be used when providers are recording a different learning aim to that actually being delivered because the actual aim is not yet available on the LARS database. This code must not be used if the learning aim is completed or in the end of year returns.

Title: Offenders in custody with mainstream funding
Description: Learning and skills provision delivered to offenders in a custodial setting funded by mainstream funding.

Title: Progression Funding
Description: This code indicates Entry or Level 1 vocational and personal and social development learning aims that should be fully funded as the learner has not yet achieved their first full level 2 but needs a step up from basic skills in order to progress to this level.

Title: Access to Apprenticeships
Description: This code is used to identify learners on an Access to Apprenticeship pathway

Title: Group Training Association (GTA)
Description: A GTA provides support on accessing, managing and the delivery of training for a group of employers to encourage their involvement in apprenticeships. This code is used to monitor the delivery of apprenticeships through GTAs.

Title: Apprenticeship Training Agency (ATA)
Description: This code is used to identify Apprenticeships where the apprentice is directly employed by the ATA who then hires them out to a host employer.

Title: NEET Apprenticeship starts
Description:
This code is used to identify all learners from the NEET cohort who start an apprenticeship from 2011/12 through to 2014/15. This will allow reporting on the impact of the funding available to support this group and the progress toward the 40,000 target.

NEET status applies where someone who is eligible for funding (2011/12 Learner Eligibility and Contribution Rules) has not been enrolled or engaged on any educational courses or training programmes or been employed under a contract of employment for a period of a minimum of 13 weeks prior to starting the apprenticeship. Other programmes may have different definitions of NEET.

Title: Apprenticeship Grant for Employers for 16 to 24 year olds (AGE 16-24) - Provider Payment Trigger
Description: This code is used to identify learners recruited to apprenticeships through AGE 16 to 24 and importantly it will also act as the trigger for payment to providers claiming the grant on behalf of eligible employers. This is the Employer Wage Inventive Initiative as part of the Youth Contract. The National Apprenticeship Service will provide up to 40,000 Apprenticeships for Small Employers recruiting 16 to 24 year olds with a value of £1,500 to encourage new employers to take on new 16- 24 year old apprentices.  More information available at http://www.apprenticeships.org.uk/Partners/Policy/AGE-1624.aspx

Title: Apprenticeship Grant for Employers for 16 to 24 year olds (AGE 16 to 24) - Strategic Partner
Description: This code is used to identify learners recruited to apprenticeships through AGE 16 to 24 where payment to the employer will be made via our Strategic Partners - providers should use this code where they are reporting the learner for MI and performance reasons, but not claiming an AGE 16 to 24 Grant payment on behalf of the employer.   More information available at http://www.apprenticeships.org.uk/Partners/Policy/AGE-1624.aspx

Title: Greater Manchester Commitment to Youth Employment
Description: A grant scheme for 16-24 year old unemployed or NEET young people in Greater Manchester who start new apprenticeships. It is offered with an optional pre-apprenticeship trial.

Title: Norfolk Apprenticeships Subsidy
Description: This code should be used to identify learners who are undertaking apprenticeship programmes where employers are eligible for Norfolk County Council wage subsidy - rate dependent on age & prior attainment of learner. All eligible apprentices must be resident in Norfolk

Title: Mandation to Skills Training
Description: This code should be used to identify learners who have been referred (mandated) by DWP to training if an individual has a skills needs that is a barrier to work.

Title: Employer Ownership Pilot - Employer defined programme
Description: This code should be used by providers participating in the Employer Ownership Pilot to identify learning aims that a learner is undertaking as part of a programme that is similar to an apprenticeship but which does not meet all the requirements of a SASE framework.

Title: 14-16 Direct Funded Students in FE
Description: This code should be used to identify learners from September 2013 who are full time 14 and 15 year olds and have been recruited directly by a General Further Education (GFE) or Sixth Form college.

Title: 14-16 Home Educated Students
Description: This code should be used to identify learners from September 2013 who are alternative provision census home educated students. These students will be funded as 14-16s direct in FE.

Title: Residential Courses
Description: To identify courses that are being delivered by Long Term Residential Colleges (LTRCs) to learners in residence. The Skills Funding Agency funding calculation will use this information.

Title: Personalised learning programme for learners with learning difficulties and/or disabilities
Description: This code should be used to identify non regulated provision that is created for learners with learning difficulties and/or disabilities as part of their personalised learning programme where there is no other suitable alternative within a regulated framework

Title: Skills Made Easy-Sheffield City Deal
Description: Sheffield City Council management of a skills brokerage and delivery service offering apprenticeships and workplace up-skilling across the Sheffield City Region. The code will be used to identify this distinct provision amongst the regular ILR returns by Sheffield City Council. This is for monitoring and performance management purposes and so that Skills Funding Agency can make accurate reports back to BIS.

Title: RoTL
Description: This code should be used for learners who are released from prison on a temporary licence.

Title: ESOL Plus (Mandation)
Description: The code for ESOL Plus (Mandation) Funding is only to be used by providers selected to receive an allocation in order to deliver ESOL provision to improve the speaking and listening skills of JCP mandated learners to Entry Level 1 or Entry Level 2 in a DWP priority area. Providers delivering this additional provision will have already received further information from the Agency on the use of these funds.

Title: Skills Training Innovation and Employment (STRIVE) pilot for the homeless
Description: Three year pre employment and skills pilot to support people who are homeless in London and furthest from the labour market to start to prepare for work. The pilot will be targeted at people who are homeless claiming JSA or ESA and not on the Work Programme. The pilot will provide intensive English and maths support to claimants who are placed on the ‘Help to Work’ scheme.

Title: Princes Trust Team programme
Description:
This code should be used to record learning aims that are delivered as part of the Prince’s Trust TEAM programme. TEAM is a 12-week personal development course, offering work experience, qualifications, practical skills, community projects and a residential week.
It should be used for both 16-19 and Adult skills funded learners.

Title: Apprenticeship Grant for Employers for 16 to 24 year olds (AGE 16 to 24) - Provider Payment Trigger from  1 Jan 2015 to 31 Mar 2016
Description:
This code is used to identify learners recruited to apprenticeships through the Apprenticeship Grant for Employers 16 to 24 (AGE) from 1 January 2015 to 31 December 2015 and will act as the trigger for payment to providers claiming the grant on behalf of eligible employers. 

The grant applies only to apprenticeship Frameworks and not to the new apprenticeship standards.

Title: Apprenticeship Grant for Employers for 16 to 24 year olds (AGE 16 to 24) - Strategic Partner from 1 Jan 2015 to 31 Dec 2015
Description:
This code is used to identify learners recruited to apprenticeships through AGE 16 to 24 where payment to the employer will be made via our Strategic Partners - providers should use this code where they are reporting the learner for MI and performance reasons, but not claiming an AGE 16 to 24 Grant payment on behalf of the employer.   

The grant applies only to apprenticeship Frameworks and not to the new apprenticeship standards.

Title: ESF AGE Enhancement 1-49
Description: Delivery of enhancements to the Apprenticeship Grant for Employers through Priority 2 ESF funding to support the development of the employer’s workforce for companies with 1-49 employees.

Title: ESF AGE Enhancements 50-249
Description: Delivery of enhancements to the Apprenticeship Grant for Employers through Priority 2 ESF funding to support the development of the employer’s workforce for companies with 50-249 employees.

Title: Community learning mental health pilot
Description: The pilot is for the development, delivery, evaluation and sharing of educational approaches to support recovery from mental health problems, in order to identify the potential for sustainability and wider dissemination.

Title: BBC Make it Digital Traineeship Programme
Description: This code should be used to record activity under the BBC Make it Digital traineeship programme. This is a national traineeship programme designed by the BBC, and delivered by a group of training providers across the country,  that aims to prepare young people for an apprenticeship or job in the digital media sector.

Title: Greater Manchester Skills for Employment Pilot Programme (Growth Deal)
Description: This code should be used to record activity under the tendered Greater Manchester Skills for Employment Pilot Programme (Growth Deal), delivered by selected providers, for payment reconciliation.  The code should only be used for the growth deal element (funding code 81) and not for the ESF element (funding code 70).

Title: Sheffield City Region Skills Bank (Growth Deal)
Description: This code should be used to record activity under the tendered Sheffield City Region Skills Bank (Growth Deal), delivered by selected providers, for payment reconciliation.  The code should only be used for the Growth Deal element (funding code 81) and not for the ESF element (funding code 70).

Title: Armed forces leavers
Description:
This code should be used to identify learning aims for Armed forces leavers, aged 24 or older, studying their first full level 2 or 3 qualification.

Armed forces leavers are:
 - those who left the British armed forces in the past 10 years after completing four or more years of service, or
 - those who have been medically discharged from the British armed forces due to an injury in active service, after completing basic training.

Title: Steel Industries Redundancy Training
Description: This code should be used to identify learning aims that are being undertaken as part of the package of support that has been agreed for learners who have been made redundant or who are at risk of redundancy from the Steel Industry. This code must only be used by providers who have received written permission from the Agency to do so.

Title: Apprenticeship seasonal worker pilot
Description: This code should be used to identify learning aims that are being delivered as part of the seasonal worker pilot where the apprenticeship is completed in more than a single episode of employment/learning.

Title: Apprenticeship Grant for Employers (AGE) - Provider Payment Trigger from 1 April 2016 to 31 July 2017
Description:
This code is used to identify learners recruited to apprenticeships through the Apprenticeship Grant for Employers 16 to 24 (AGE) from 1 April 2016 to 31 July 2017 and will act as the trigger for payment to providers claiming the grant on behalf of eligible employers. 

This code should also be used for learners / employers who fall within a devolved area. 

The grant applies only to apprenticeship Frameworks and not to the new apprenticeship standards.

Title: Non-apprenticeship workplace learning originally funded under the employer responsive funding model
Description:
This code is used to identify learning aims that are not part of an apprenticeship programme and were previously funded under Funding Model 45. This code should be used for aims that meet all of the following criteria: 
 - Learning start date before 1 August 2013
 - AND Funding model = 35
 - AND NOT part of an apprenticeship programme (ProgType is not 2,3,10,20,21,22 or 23)
 - AND EITHER LearnDelFAMType = WPL (in the 2015 to 2016 ILR) OR Learning delivery monitoring code = LDM125

Title: Former Employer Ownership Pilot Provision
Description: This code is used to identify learning aims previously delivered through the Employer Ownership Pilot and which is eligible for mainstream funding to be recorded on the ILR. This code will be used to exclude these records from analysis to prevent double-counting.

Title: HESA-generated ILR file
Description:
This code is used to identify records generated by HESA. 
This code is used only by HESA and is auto generated whilst creating an ILR record from a HESA return.

Title: Non-apprenticeship Sporting Excellence Award
Description: This will permit providers to identify those learners and associated qualifications with alternative completion conditions (ACC) and permit them to be funded on a bespoke basis whilst ensuring they remain outside MI/SFR and other considerations that would make them formally “apprentices” whilst still undertaking the qualification within that framework.

Title: Non-apprenticeship Theatre and live events
Description: Apprenticeship “Theatre and live events” which relates to a variety of possible apprenticeship routes described in the Act of Parliament

Title: Non-apprenticeship Sea Fishing
Description: Apprenticeship “Sea Fishing” which relates to a variety of possible apprenticeship routes described in the Act of Parliament

Title: Apprenticeship being delivered to own employees
Description: This code is used to identify apprenticeship programmes on Funding model 36 that are being delivered by an employer to their own employees, and so do not require co-investment payments to be recorded on the ILR.

Title: Procured Adult Education Budget (AEB)
Description:
This code should be used for all learning aims on the standalone procured AEB contracts that were awarded in July 2017 and started in November 2017. This code is required to distinguish procured AEB from the allocated AEB in the 2017/18 contract extensions that started in August 2017. The code is used by the funding calculation and mis-coding will affect payments.
The contracts are for up to three years but are reviewed annually.
AEB combines all ESFA Adult participation and support funding that is not European Social Fund, advanced learner loans or apprenticeships.

Title: Extended Allocated Non-Levy Apprenticeships
Description: This LDM code is to identify starts on the May 2017 to December 2017 allocated non-levy apprenticeships delivery, where the contracts are extended to March 2018 for new starts. Only existing non-levy providers who were unsuccessful in the procurement exercise can use this code and enrol new starts up to March 2018.  The ESFA will monitor the use of the code, if validation rules cannot be applied, and ineligible starts will not be funded.  This code must not be used on procured non-levy contracts.  The code must be used across all collections/academic years until the apprentice has completed.

Title: Career Learning Pilot
Description: This code should be used to identify learners who are part of the career learning pilot.

Title: Flexible Learning Fund
Description: This code should be used to identify those providers who are part of a successful bid for the Fund and will be delivering eligible provision to AEB or Loans learners using flexible approaches and/or packages of learning.

Title: Waiver to record payment records for apprenticeships
Description: This code should be used to identify providers who have authorisation to waive recording payment records for Apprenticeships.

Title: Apprentice care leavers
Description: This code should be used to identify apprentices who do not wish their employer to be informed that they are a care leaver.

Title: Learners in receipt of low wages
Description: This code is used to fully fund eligible co-funded learners who earn less than £15,736.50 annual gross salary. This is based on the Social Mobility Commission’s low pay threshold of £8.07 (hourly rate in 2016) and on the assumption of a 37.5 hour contract with paid statutory holiday entitlement.
